children.heading: President; preparation of plan and submission to Congress of report
children.label: a President; preparation of plan and submission to Congress of report
A
children.text: policy analysis and decision determination within the Executive Office of the President;

children.label: B
children.text: continuing long-range analysis of materials use to meet national security, economic, industrial and social needs; the adequacy and stability of supplies; and the industrial and economic implications of supply shortages or disruptions;

children.label: C
children.text: continuing private sector consultation in Federal materials programs; and

children.label: D
children.text: interagency coordination at the level of the President’s Cabinet;

recommendations for the collection, analysis, and dissemination of information concerning domestic and international long-range materials demand, supply and needs, including consideration of the establishment of a separate materials information agency patterned after the Bureau of Labor Statistics; and

recommendations for legislation and administrative initiatives necessary to reconcile policy conflicts and to establish programs and institutional structures necessary to achieve the goals of a national materials policy.

children.heading: Director of Office of Science and Technology Policy; coordination, etc., activities
children.label: b Director of Office of Science and Technology Policy; coordination, etc., activities

children.text: through the National Science and Technology Council coordinate Federal materials research and development and related activities in accordance with the policies and objectives established in this chapter;

place special emphasis on the long-range assessment of national materials needs related to scientific and technological concerns and the research and development, Federal and private, necessary to meet those needs; and

prepare an assessment of national materials needs related to scientific and technological changes over the next five years. Such assessment shall be revised on an annual basis. Where possible, the Director shall extend the assessment in 10- and 25-year increments over the whole expected lifetime of such needs and technologies.

children.heading: Secretary of Commerce; consultative, etc., requirements; identification and assessment activities
children.label: c Secretary of Commerce; consultative, etc., requirements; identification and assessment activities

children.text: not later than 1 year after , submit to the Congress a report that assesses critical materials needs and that recommends programs that would assist in meeting such needs, including an assessment of economic stockpiles; andDecember 27, 2020
assess the adequacy and stability of the supply of materials necessary to maintain national security, economic well-being, public health, and industrial production.

children.heading: Secretary of Defense and other Cabinet members; assessment, etc., activities
children.label: d Secretary of Defense and other Cabinet members; assessment, etc., activities
children.text: The Secretary of Defense, together with such other members of the Cabinet as are deemed necessary by the President, shall prepare a report assessing critical materials needs related to national security and identifying the steps necessary to meet those needs. The report shall include an assessment of the Defense Production Act of 1950 (50 U.S.C. App. 2061 et seq.) [now et seq.], and the Strategic and Critical Materials Stock Piling Act (50 U.S.C. App. 98 et seq.) [ et seq.]. Such report shall be made available to the Congress within 1 year after , and shall be revised periodically as deemed necessary.50 U.S.C. 450150 U.S.C. 98December 27, 2020

children.heading: Secretary of the Interior; initiation of actions; report
children.label: e Secretary of the Interior; initiation of actions; report

children.text: improve the capacity of the United States Geological Survey to assess international minerals supplies;
increase the level of mining and metallurgical research by the United States Geological Survey in critical and strategic minerals; and
improve the availability and analysis of mineral data in Federal land use decisionmaking.

children.heading: Secretary of the Interior; collection, evaluation, and analysis activities concerning information
children.label: f Secretary of the Interior; collection, evaluation, and analysis activities concerning information
children.text: In furtherance of the policies of this chapter, the Secretary of the Interior shall collect, evaluate, and analyze information concerning mineral occurrence, production, and use from industry, academia, and Federal and State agencies. Notwithstanding the provisions of , data and information provided to the Department by persons or firms engaged in any phase of mineral or mineral-material production or large-scale consumption shall not be disclosed outside of the Department of the Interior in a nonaggregated form so as to disclose data and information supplied by a single person or firm, unless there is no objection to the disclosure of such data and information by the donor: , That the Secretary may disclose nonaggregated data and information to Federal defense agencies, or to the Congress upon official request for appropriate purposes.section 552 of title 5Provided, however
